2603.23994.title: Understanding the Challenges in Iterative Generative Optimization with LLMs
2603.23994.paper_summary: Generative optimization uses large language models (LLMs) to iteratively improve artifacts (such as code, workflows or prompts) using execution feedback. It is a promising approach to building self-improving agents, yet in practice remains brittle: despite active research, only 9% of surveyed agents used any automated optimization. We argue that this brittleness arises because, to set up a learning loop, an engineer must make ``hidden'' design choices: What can the optimizer edit and what is the "right" learning evidence to provide at each update? We investigate three factors that affect most applications: the starting artifact, the credit horizon for execution traces, and batching trials and errors into learning evidence. Through case studies in MLAgentBench, Atari, and BigBench Extra Hard, we find that these design decisions can determine whether generative optimization succeeds, yet they are rarely made explicit in prior work. Different starting artifacts determine which solutions are reachable in MLAgentBench, truncated traces can still improve Atari agents, and larger minibatches do not monotonically improve generalization on BBEH. We conclude that the lack of a simple, universal way to set up learning loops across domains is a major hurdle for productionization and adoption. We provide practical guidance for making these choices.
2603.23994.paper_summary_zh: 生成優化利用大型語言模型（LLMs）透過執行反饋迭代改善工件（如代碼、工作流程或提示）。這是一種有前景的自我改進代理構建方法，但在實踐中仍然脆弱：儘管有積極的研究，只有9%的受訪代理使用了任何自動化優化。我們認為這種脆弱性產生的原因是，為了設置學習循環，工程師必須做出“隱藏”的設計選擇：優化器可以編輯什麼，以及在每次更新時提供什麼是“正確”的學習證據？我們調查了影響大多數應用的三個因素：起始工件、執行痕跡的信用範圍，以及將試驗和錯誤批量化為學習證據。通過在MLAgentBench、Atari和BigBench Extra Hard的案例研究，我們發現這些設計決策可以決定生成優化是否成功，但在先前的工作中這些決策很少被明確提出。不同的起始工件決定了在MLAgentBench中可以達到的解決方案，截斷的痕跡仍然可以改善Atari代理，而較大的小批量不會單調改善BBEH的泛化。我們得出結論，缺乏一種簡單、通用的方式在各個領域設置學習循環是生產化和採用的一個主要障礙。我們提供了在做出這些選擇時的實用指導。
2603.23994.author: Allen Nie et.al.
2603.23994.authors: Allen Nie, Xavier Daull, Zhiyi Kuang, Abhinav Akkiraju, Anish Chaudhuri, Max Piasevoli, Ryan Rong, YuCheng Yuan, Prerit Choudhary, Shannon Xiao, Rasool Fakoor, Adith Swaminathan, Ching-An Cheng
